other_observers: Steve Stahoviach or however he spells his name, Glenn Walbek, Joey Kellner, Joe Roller, Larry Modesitt, Nathan Pieplow, Andrew Spencer.; First found at Holbrook Reservoir about 3 hours earlier by Brandon Percival, Mark Peterson, Brad and Brian Steger.
description: Ths is the bird first located and identified at Holbrook Reservoir earlier in the day.  It was a very large tern, identified as a tern by the the tern-like jizz that includes quicky jerky flight over water, small dagger-like bill, pointed wings.   The bird was dark gray on the dorsal surface. including the back, top of head (except the forehead) bill and tail. The undersides with white except for the dark contrasting primaries, giving the bird a Swainson's Hawk-like wing coloration underneath. The bill was all dark. The front of the head, between the cap and the bill was very contrasty white. The face was white with a noticeable dark eye"lore" extenting from the dark ey to the base of the bill, which was seen only at closest range (at least by me.)   The breast was imaculate white, turning slightly gray posteriorly. The feet were never visible. The tail was FORKED, and was dark both on the dorsal and ventral surface. Although I knew to look, I didn't look for the white edging to the tail. The bird did not have a light hindcollar as in Bridled Tern.Behaviors: This bird was a very strong flier, flying mostly 50-80 feet above the water, but on at least two occasions it made dives to the water surface, but never plunge-dived, rather it seemed to pick something from the water surface. It flew non-stoop for about 30 minutes and then seemed to disappear.Call: none heardPlumage: adult
